english: po., prop. ćaṅ-du (cang du), postp. c. accus., to, with, Koṅ ćaṅ-du mi ₒgro (kong cang du mi 'gro) I do not go to him Mil., Pth.; ṅa ćaṅ-du (nga cang du) with me, in my presence Mil. The word seems to be rather obsolete; more recent editions having gaṅ-du (gang du) and druṅ-du (drung du) instead of it.
(?) W. the green shell of a walnut.

wylie: cang ce, cang cer, cang ne
english: Sch.; a small bowl or dish; Cs.: continually.

1. Cs. slow; Lex. ćám-gyis jog (cam gyis jog), and several other passages, the sense of which is not quite clear; cf. ćam-mé (cam me). - 2. glistening, glittering (?) cf. lćam-mé (lcam me). - 3. W. whole, unimpaired, *sa* (*sa*) (lit. rtsva (rtsva)) *ćam-mé yod* (*cam me yod*) the whole store of hay is still left (entire).
Ld. a bunch of flowers, sprigs etc., a handful of ears of corn.

1. Lex. ćar-ré (car re), Cs. ća-ré (ca re), Sch. also ćar-már (car mar), always, continually Cs. - 2. also ćar (car), ćár-du (car du), with numerals, esp. gćig-ćar (gcig car) at the same time, simultaneously, opp. to one after the other, successively (viz. doing or suffering a thing, sleeping, dying etc.) Dzl.; at once, on a sudden, opp. to gradually Mil.; lṅá-ćar (lnga car) all the five together Thgy., gnyís-ćar (gnyis car), drúg-ćar (drug car) etc.
Cs.: 'noise, ćal-ćal (cal cal) id.; ćal-rgyúg (cal rgyug) rumour, (false) report'; ćal-ćòl (cal col) idle talk, nonsense, ćal-ćòl gtam (cal col gtam) id. Mil.

I. interr. pron. in direct questions: 1. what? (C. gen. gaṅ (gang) instead of ći (ci)) ći śes (ci shes) (like the Hind. क्या जाने) who knows? col. W.; also pleon. at the end of a question after the ... am: ṅa ṅó-śes-sam ći? (nga ngo shes sam ci?) do you know me? do you? Dzl.; ćii (ci'i) of whom? whose? followed by phyir (phyir), don (don), ćed (ced), slad (-du) (slad (-du)): why? wherefore? inst. of ćii phyir (ci'i phyir) also ći-phyir (ci phyir) etc.; de ćii phyir zé-na (de ci'i phyir ze na) 'this wherefore? (= why this?) if so it is asked'. (This phrase, besides the gerundial particles - esp. pas (pas) - is the only way in which in B. the causal conjunction 'for' (Lat. nam, enim) can be expressed, and in translating into Tibetan, the English conjunction must therefore often be altogether omitted.) ćii ₒbrás-bu (ci'i 'bras bu) what sort of fruit? ći ri (ci ri) what kind of a mountain? i.e. of what consisting? Pth.; ći (ci) also, like an adj., is placed after the word to which it belongs: rgyu ći-las (rgyu ci las) for what reason? on what account? Thgy. - 2. why? wherefore? but only in negative questions: bdág-la des ći ma ćog (bdag la des ci ma cog) why should not that suffice me? Thgy.; ći mi sgrub (ci mi sgrub) why do you not procure...? inst. of the imp. procure! Mil.; bsám-na ći ma legs (bsam na ci ma legs) if you considered..., why would not that be a good thing? = you had better consider, you ought to consider Mil.; frq.: de ₒbyuṅ-na ći ma ruṅ (de 'byung na ci ma rung) if that happened, why should it not be desirable? = would that it happened! oh, may it happen! - 3. how? in conjunction with other words, v. below. - 4. inst. of a note of interrogation, e.g. in: ći gnaṅ (ci gnang), for gnaṅ-ṅam (gnang ngam), gśégs-par ći gnaṅ (gshegs par ci gnang) do you allow(me) to come? Dzl. ~ V, 13; ~ S, 5. II. correlatively: which, what; whatsoever; every thing, much like gaṅ (gang), q.v., esp. the syntactical explanations given there. ći (ci), as a correlative, ought prop. always to be written ji (ji), yet not even in decidedly correlative sentences is this strictly observed: ći byed(-na- aṅ) (ci byed(-na- ang)) whatever I may do Glr.; ći bgyi bka nyan(-te) ṅéd-kyis bsgrub (ci bgyi bka nyan(-te) nged kyis bsgrub) whatever we may be bidden to do, we shall obediently perform Pth.; ći myur (ci myur), also ći myur źíg-la (ci myur zhig la) Pth. as quick as possible;
